About
Cookies are small text files that websites use to help make a user’s experience more efficient.
We may store cookies on your device if they are strictly necessary for the operation of this website. For all other types of cookies, we require your consent.  

This website uses different types of cookies. Some cookies are set by third-party services that appear on our pages.  

You can change or withdraw your consent at any time via the Cookie Declaration on our website.
For more information about who we are, how to contact us, and how we process personal data, please see our Privacy Policy.  

When contacting us regarding your consent, please include your consent ID and the date.

# Who we are
[![](https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Arrow-White.png)](https://kiutra.com/company/#mission)
COMPANY
## Who we are
[![](https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Arrow-White.png)](https://kiutra.com/company/#mission)
## We unlock the full potential of quantum technologies with our pioneering cooling solutions
Quantum computing has the potential to address complex challenges in areas like drug development, battery technology and climate modeling. It will also enhance sensing capabilities, supporting applications in medical imaging and enabling physically secure quantum communication.  
  
Quantum devices can already be fabricated in large quantities using state-of-the-art thin-film deposition technology. However, developing and operating these devices requires extreme environments at temperatures well below 1 K (–272 °C). Dilution refrigerators usually generate these sub-kelvin temperatures using helium-3. This method is challenging and requires experienced personnel and complex infrastructure. As a result, dilution refrigeration leads to tedious low-temperature investigations and slow feedback on device properties and quality. This slows down quantum R&D. 
The scarcity of helium-3 adds further risk. Helium-3 is one of the rarest resources on Earth and is produced in small volumes from radioactive tritium decay in mostly military facilities in North America and Russia. 
Its extreme rarity and strategic relevance have even prompted considerations to mine helium-3 on the Moon, where it is embedded in the lunar rock. This limited availability threatens the sovereign use of quantum technologies and will hinder their advancement and adoption at industrial scale.
Because we believe that cryogenics should accelerate quantum technologies, not hold it back, we have dedicated ourselves to setting a new standard. This standard is characterized by high performance, fast delivery, absolute reliability and unmatched efficiency. Central to this is the use of a fundamentally different technology in our cryostats. To reach sub-kelvin temperatures, we cool with paramagnetic solids in a process known as [Adiabatic Demagnetization Refrigeration (ADR)](https://kiutra.com/technology/#adr).
ADR is well established, but we are the only company worldwide that can offer it in a continuous configuration (cADR). With this, we provide the only alternative to helium-3-based cooling. Magnetic cooling has several advantages. Our cutting-edge cooling solutions are easy-to-use – you don’t need to hire a low-temperature physicist to run your cryostat. The systems are modular and fast, resulting in time, energy and cost savings.
![graphic explaining the company name: "Q or kju" the symbol for heat and "trahere" the latin for draw or extract, combining to make the name kiutra.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20737%20475'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
## Advanced cooling solutions 
### Enabling the development, characterization and testing as well as the operation of quantum systems
##### L-Type Rapid
The [L-Type Rapid](https://kiutra.com/products/l-type-rapid-cryostat/) offers easy access to the kelvin and sub-kelvin range and delivers the fastest testing and characterization on the market, combining high sample throughput with ease of use.
##### X-Type
The [X-Type](https://kiutra.com/products/x-type/) is a modular, helium-3-free cooling architecture designed specifically for scalable quantum systems.
![L-Type Rapid fast characterization cryostat being loaded with a sample
](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201024%20536'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
##### Specialized systems
In addition, we develop cryogenic environments for specialized use cases. 
This includes the [S-Type](https://kiutra.com/products/cryostats-for-specialized-use-cases/#s-type-essential), a rack-integrated sub-kelvin platform for continuous cooling, and the T-Type, an optical kelvin temperature cryostat optimized for trapped-ion chips.
##### Cryogenic Services
To enable you to get fast testing and characterization results without the need for your own facility, we provide an on-demand [service](https://kiutra.com/services).
You just need to send in a sample, and our dedicated low-temperature experts will then handle the entire process. This ensures cost-efficiency and swift, 100% reliable results for you. 
Alternatively, set up and run your own experiments in our Characterization Lab, where we operate four dedicated L-Type Rapid cryostats alongside state-of-the-art measurement electronics.
## Shaping the future of quantum technologies
### We actively push innovation through research projects in cryogenics and quantum technologies
In 2024, we began work on two major R&D projects: LEMON and CRYOFAST. 
[LEMON](https://kiutra.com/projects/large-scale-magnetic-cooling/) focuses on developing a scalable, helium-3-free cryogenic cooling solution capable of reaching milli-Kelvin temperatures. By pushing the limits of cADR, LEMON will meet the increasing cooling demands of quantum technologies, especially in quantum computing. 
[CRYOFAST](https://kiutra.com/projects/cryofast-democratizing-access-to-sub-kelvin-temperatures/) aims to democratize access to sub-Kelvin temperatures by providing researchers and developers with professional testing and characterization services at a centralized facility for advanced cryogenic measurements on sensitive devices.
We also collaborate with world-class partners, participating in numerous national and European research consortia. Our role in these initiatives involves developing and deploying integrated cryo-operating platforms. 
![Conducting a measurement on the cryo-floor ](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201024%20536'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
Through our products and services, we cover the full spectrum of groundbreaking quantum technologies – from superconducting circuits, and ion traps for quantum computing to NV centers and single-photon detectors for sensing and communication applications. Looking ahead, our focus remains on providing systems, integrated solutions, and services across the entire quantum technology chain.

## Network and partners
### kiutra is part of a strong network
## NIKI Glass Co. LTD
Our distribution partner for Japan is [Niki Glass](http://www.nikiglass.co.jp/english/New/index.html). Established in 1923, they specialize in advanced measurement instruments and pursue R&D of new technologies.
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20300%20234'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

ESA Business Incubation Center
kiutra has successfully completed the renowned incubation program of [European Space Agency (ESA) BIC Bavaria](https://esa-bic.de/). ESA BIC guides entrepreneurs and startups in turning their ideas and inventions into successful businesses, promoting pioneering companies with disruptive products and digital businesses.
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20300%20234'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
Quantum Business Network
The [Quantum Business Network |QBN>](https://quantumbusinessnetwork.de/) brings together people from academia, industry and politics to accelerate technological advancements through collaborations and exchange between universities and industry.
Kiutra is proud to be a member of this Network and to actively contribute to events and workshops fostering the collaboration on quantum technologies between science, politics, and industry.
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20300%20234'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
Cryogenic Society of Europe
kiutra is a corporate member of the [Cryogenic Society of Europe (CSE)](https://www.cryoeurope.org/). Founded 2015 as a non-profit organization, the CSE strengthens the European position of cryogenic technologies, promotes communication and information exchange in this area, fosters the training of young researchers, and brings together those active in cryogenic technologies and potential user communities.
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20300%20234'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
Quantum Industry Consortium
kiutra is one of the initial members of the [Quantum Industry Consortium (QuIC)](https://qt.eu/about-quantum-flagship/the-quantum-flagship-community/quic/). The QuIC is part of the European Quantum Flagship program and aims on promoting and fostering the common interests of European industry partners towards a broad application of Quantum Technologies, e.g., by identifying needs and shortages in critical sectors such as the supply chains, enabling components and technologies, or intellectual property.
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20300%20234'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
Cryogenic Society of America
kiutra is a corporate sustaining member of the [Cryogenic Society of America (CSA)](https://cryogenicsociety.org/). Formed in 1964, the CSA is a non-profit technical society serving all those interested in cryogenics, e.g., by promoting research and development of low temperature processes, and connecting those concerned with the applications of low temperature technology.
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20300%20234'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
International Society for Sample Environment
The mission of the [International Society for Sample Environment (ISSE)](https://sampleenvironment.org/) is to strengthen the position of sample environment technologies at large-scale facilities, to foster the training of young technicians, engineers and researchers and to bring together international activities in sample environment technologies in social, scientific, industrial and political forums.
kiutra is happy to be part of this unique society and is eager to learn and adapt our products to meet the specific needs of large-scale facilities.
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20300%20234'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
Aeneas
kiutra is a member of [AENEAS](https://aeneas-office.org/), the Association for European NanoElectronics ActivitieS. As an industrial association, established in 2006, AENAES wants to promote Research, Development and Innovation in order to strengthen the competitiveness of European industry across the electronics components and systems value chain.
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20300%20234'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
ASK Corporation
Our distribution partner for Korea is [ASK ](https://askcorp.co.kr/)(Applied Science Korea) Corporation. Established in 1999, they specialize in cryogenic, magnetic, quantum and vacuum technologies, serving universities, governmental R&D institutes and industrial labs.
## Timeline
###  2018 
Established as a spin-off from the Technical University Munich
_EXIST Transfer of Research_ program
_ESA Business Incubation_ program
###  2019 
Seed financing round
Awarded BMBF grant for the _MARQUAND_ project with Walter Schottky Institute
Opening of production and office facility in Munich, Obersendling
First rapid characterization cryostat installed
###  2020 
Awarded EIC Accelerator grant for  _CADR_ project
First optical cryostat installed
Awarded Eurostars grant for  _SPROUT_ project
###  2021 
Series-A financing round
Awarded ECSEL JU grant for  _MATQu_ project
_INTEL Ignite_ incubation program
###  2022 
Awarded BMBF grant for  _MUNIQC-SC_ project
First trapped ion cryostat installed
First cryostat installed in the USA
First service measurement performed
Move to larger production space and office in Mittersendling, Munich
###  2023 
Awarded EU grant for  _MILLENION_ project
S-Type Optical 2023 model
50+ employees
###  2024 
L-Type Rapid Sample Puck 55 released
ASK Corporation becomes the distribution partner for Korea, and NIKI GLASS Ltd becomes the distribution partner for Japan
Awarded EIC Accelerator grant for  _CRYOFAST_ project
Awarded EIC Pathfinder grant for  _LEMON_ project 
Awarded BMBF grant for _QuantumSPICE_ project
###  2025 
€13 million financing round to eliminate bottlenecks in quantum supply chains
Characterization Lab expanded as part of the _CRYOFAST_ project
kiutra.io – smarter cryostat control released
L-Type Rapid temperature range extended to 50 mK – 300 K
## Sustainability
As scientists, we want to shape the world in a positive way and by taking a responsible approach to business operations, we want to do this as sustainably as we can. We have taken our first steps in assessing and reducing our greenhouse gas emissions (GHG) and see it as an essential area for continuous assessment and improvement.
![abstract colorful drawing of a tree](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20601%201024'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
**Climate impact pilot program**
In 2022, we took part in a pilot program to model the climate impact of one of our cryostats compared to regular cryogenic equipment on the market today. The pilot showed that one L-Type Rapid cryostat can save up to 70% in energy use for a quick test (assuming 1 hour of testing time at 100 mK).
Importantly we also identified reduction of the GHG emissions from our electricity consumption as a top measure to implement at kiutra. We subsequently switched to a 100% green electricity supplier with electricity coming entirely from environmentally friendly hydropower plants.
**Enabling quantum technologies**
We build products and services to help scientists and engineers implement modern, second-generation quantum technologies. These technologies have the potential to provide significant benefits to the environment, for example by providing more energy-efficient computing, as well as disruptive computing that could lead to improved materials for energy storage and conversion, solar cell and battery technology, and power distribution.
**Long lasting and recyclable products**
Our products are designed to last – they are durable, repairable, and upgradable. This reduces waste and saves energy and resources that would otherwise be used in the production of a brand-new cryostat or to replace a substantial part of the device.
**3D printing**
In development and various production processes we use 3D printing as a prototyping tool to cut down the production stages needed for metal parts, reducing the energy used and emissions produced to build them.
**What we are working on**
We are currently engaged in a project to help evaluate processes and to identify and quantify areas where we can lessen our environmental impact.
### Circularity in the Quantum Industry
A joint white paper by kiutra and CIRCULAR REPUBLIC
[READ THE WHITE PAPER](https://kiutra.com/download-white-paper-circularity-in-the-quantum-industry/)

type="application/ld+json">{"@context":"https:\/\/schema.org","@type":"AboutPage","mainEntityOfPage":{"@type":"WebPage","@id":"https:\/\/kiutra.com\/company\/"},"headline":"Company","description":"COMPANY\n\n\n\nWho we are\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nCOMPANY\n\n\n\nWho we are\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nWe unlock the full potential of quantum technologies with our pioneering cooling solutions\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nQuantum computing has the potential to address complex challenges in areas like drug development, battery technology and climate modeling. It will also enhance sensing capabilities, supporting applications in medical imaging and enabling physically secure quantum communication. Quantum devices can already be fabricated in large quantities using state-of-the-art thin-film deposition technology. However, developing and operating these devices requires extreme environments at temperatures well below 1 K (\u2013272 \u00b0C). Dilution refrigerators usually generate these sub-kelvin temperatures using helium-3. This method is challenging and requires experienced personnel and complex infrastructure. As a result, dilution refrigeration leads to tedious low-temperature investigations and slow feedback on device properties and quality. This slows down quantum R&D. \n\n\n\nThe scarcity of helium-3 adds further risk. Helium-3 is one of the rarest resources on Earth and is produced in small volumes from radioactive tritium decay in mostly military facilities in North America and Russia. \n\n\n\n\n\nIts extreme rarity and strategic relevance have even prompted considerations to mine helium-3 on the Moon, where it is embedded in the lunar rock. This limited availability threatens the sovereign use of quantum technologies and will hinder their advancement and adoption at industrial scale.\n\n\n\nBecause we believe that cryogenics should accelerate quantum technologies, not hold it back, we have dedicated ourselves to setting a new standard. This standard is characterized by high performance, fast delivery, absolute reliability and unmatched efficiency. Central to this is the use of a fundamentally different technology in our cryostats. To reach sub-kelvin temperatures, we cool with paramagnetic solids in a process known as Adiabatic Demagnetization Refrigeration (ADR).\n\n\n\nADR is well established, but we are the only company worldwide that can offer it in a continuous configuration (cADR). With this, we provide the only alternative to helium-3-based cooling. Magnetic cooling has several advantages. Our cutting-edge cooling solutions are easy-to-use \u2013 you don\u2019t need to hire a low-temperature physicist to run your cryostat. The systems are modular and fast, resulting in time, energy and cost savings.\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nAdvanced cooling solutions \n\n\n\nEnabling the development, characterization and testing as well as the operation of quantum systems\n\n\n\n\n\nL-Type Rapid\n\n\n\nThe L-Type Rapid offers easy access to the kelvin and sub-kelvin range and delivers the fastest testing and characterization on the market, combining high sample throughput with ease of use.\n\n\n\nX-Type\n\n\n\nThe X-Type is a modular, helium-3-free cooling architecture designed specifically for scalable quantum systems.\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nSpecialized systems\n\n\n\nIn addition, we develop cryogenic environments for specialized use cases. \n\n\n\nThis includes the S-Type, a rack-integrated sub-kelvin platform for continuous cooling, and the T-Type, an optical kelvin temperature cryostat optimized for trapped-ion chips.\n\n\n\nCryogenic Services\n\n\n\nTo enable you to get fast testing and characterization results without the need for your own facility, we provide an on-demand service.\n\n\n\nYou just need to send in a sample, and our dedicated low-temperature experts will then handle the entire process. This ensures cost-efficiency and swift, 100% reliable results for you. \n\n\n\nAlternatively, set up and run your own experiments in our Characterization Lab, where we operate four dedicated L-Type Rapid cryostats alongside state-of-the-art measurement electronics.\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nShaping the future of quantum technologies\n\n\n\nWe actively push innovation through research projects in cryogenics and quantum technologies\n\n\n\n\n\nIn 2024, we began work on two major R&D projects: LEMON and CRYOFAST. \n\n\n\nLEMON focuses on developing a scalable, helium-3-free cryogenic cooling solution capable of reaching milli-Kelvin temperatures. By pushing the limits of cADR, LEMON will meet the increasing cooling demands of quantum technologies, especially in quantum computing. \n\n\n\nCRYOFAST aims to democratize access to sub-Kelvin temperatures by providing researchers and developers with professional testing and characterization services at a centralized facility for advanced cryogenic measurements on sensitive devices.\n\n\n\nWe also collaborate with world-class partners, participating in numerous national and European research consortia. Our role in these initiatives involves developing and deploying integrated cryo-operating platforms. \n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nThrough our products and services, we cover the full spectrum of groundbreaking quantum technologies - from superconducting circuits, and ion traps for quantum computing to NV centers and single-photon detectors for sensing and communication applications. Looking ahead, our focus remains on providing systems, integrated solutions, and services across the entire quantum technology chain.\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nLeadership\n\n\n\nMeet our management team\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nDr. Alexander RegnatCEO & Co-founder\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nDr. Tomek SchulzCOO & Co-founder\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nJan SpallekCTO & Co-founder\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nBoard and advisors\n\n\n\nTrusted deeptech investors and outstanding scientific advisors\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nChristian ZiachPrincipal \/ Authorized Signatory,High-Tech Gr\u00fcnderfonds Board Member\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nCarlo Germano RavinaManaging Director \/ Board Member, NovaCapitalBoard Member\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nDr. Michael JobstPrincipal,55 NorthBoard Member\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nDr. Wolfgang NeubertGeneral Partner,APEX VenturesBoard Member\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nUlrich KruseInvestment Manager,TRUMPF VentureBoard Member\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nLucas LaibCOO,Verve VenturesBoard Observer\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nDr. Ion HauerPrincipal,APEX Ventures\n\n\n\nBoard Observer\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nDr. Owen LozmanManaging Partner,55 North\n\n\n\nBoard Observer\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nMarco CostantiniAssociate,NovaCapital\n\n\n\nBoard Observer\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nChristiane SchumacherCFO,TRUMPF Venture\n\n\n\nBoard Observer\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nProf. Christian PfleidererProfessorship Topology of Correlated Systems,TUM School of Natural SciencesScientific Advisor & Co-founder\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nThe coolest office in Munich\n\n\n\nMeet our team, explore our facilities and take a peek at cryostats being built\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nSupporters\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nOur investors\n\n\n\n[supporters]\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nOur public funders\n\n\n\n[supporters type=\"funder\"]\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nNetwork and partners\n\n\n\nkiutra is part of a strong network\n\n\n\n\n\n[partners]\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nTimeline\n\n\n\n\n\n[milestones]\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nSustainability\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nAs scientists, we want to shape the world in a positive way and by taking a responsible approach to business operations, we want to do this as sustainably as we can. We have taken our first steps in assessing and reducing our greenhouse gas emissions (GHG) and see it as an essential area for continuous assessment and improvement.\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nClimate impact pilot program\n\n\n\nIn 2022, we took part in a pilot program to model the climate impact of one of our cryostats compared to regular cryogenic equipment on the market today. The pilot showed that one L-Type Rapid cryostat can save up to 70% in energy use for a quick test (assuming 1 hour of testing time at 100 mK).\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nImportantly we also identified reduction of the GHG emissions from our electricity consumption as a top measure to implement at kiutra. We subsequently switched to a 100% green electricity supplier with electricity coming entirely from environmentally friendly hydropower plants.\n\n\n\nEnabling quantum technologies\n\n\n\nWe build products and services to help scientists and engineers implement modern, second-generation quantum technologies. These technologies have the potential to provide significant benefits to the environment, for example by providing more energy-efficient computing, as well as disruptive computing that could lead to improved materials for energy storage and conversion, solar cell and battery technology, and power distribution.\n\n\n\nLong lasting and recyclable products \n\n\n\nOur products are designed to last \u2013 they are durable, repairable, and upgradable. This reduces waste and saves energy and resources that would otherwise be used in the production of a brand-new cryostat or to replace a substantial part of the device.\n\n\n\n3D printing\n\n\n\nIn development and various production processes we use 3D printing as a prototyping tool to cut down the production stages needed for metal parts, reducing the energy used and emissions produced to build them.\n\n\n\nWhat we are working on\n\n\n\nWe are currently engaged in a project to help evaluate processes and to identify and quantify areas where we can lessen our environmental impact.\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nCircularity in the Quantum Industry\n\n\n\nA joint white paper by kiutra and CIRCULAR REPUBLIC\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\nREAD THE WHITE PAPER","publisher":{"@type":"organization","name":"kiutra GmbH","url":"https:\/\/kiutra.com"}}</script><!-- / about-page Schema optimized by Schema Pro --><style>.recentcomments a{display:inline !important;padding:0 !important;margin:0 !important;}</style>			<style id="wpsp-style-frontend"></style>

	$(window).on('load', () => {
		var lastScrollTop = 0, delta = 20;
		$(window).scroll(function(){
			// Header Visibilty
			var nowScrollTop = $(this).scrollTop();
			if(Math.abs(lastScrollTop - nowScrollTop) >= delta){
			if (nowScrollTop > lastScrollTop){ 
		 		$("header").addClass("invisible")
		 	} else {
		 		$("header").removeClass("invisible") 
			}
			if (nowScrollTop <= 0) {
				$("header").removeClass("invisible")
				lastScrollTop = 0;
			} else {
				lastScrollTop = nowScrollTop;
			}
			}
		});
	});	
</script>

<p class="has-white-color has-text-color has-link-color wp-elements-2f58f1a7ab0acd3c518c1f76190a2682 wp-block-paragraph" style="font-style:normal;font-weight:200">COMPANY</p>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading has-text-align-left has-white-color has-text-color has-link-color wp-elements-dc35df6d46bf4118d740eb7031fd6bb5" style="font-size:30px">Who we are</h2>
</div></div>



<div style="height:100px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div>



<div class="wp-block-columns is-layout-flex wp-container-core-columns-is-layout-8f761849 wp-block-columns-is-layout-flex">
<div class="wp-block-column is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow"></div>



<div class="wp-block-column is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow"><div class="wp-block-image">
<figure class="aligncenter size-full"><a href="https://kiutra.com/company/#mission"><img decoding="async" width="45" height="35" src="https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Arrow-White.png" alt="" class="wp-image-4349"></a></figure>

<div class="wp-block-cover is-light" id="mission"><span aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-cover__background has-white-background-color has-background-dim-100 has-background-dim"></span><div class="wp-block-cover__inner-container is-layout-flow wp-block-cover-is-layout-flow">
<div class="wp-block-group has-black-color has-text-color"><div class="wp-block-group__inner-container is-layout-flow wp-block-group-is-layout-flow">
<h2 class="wp-block-heading has-text-align-center">We unlock the full potential of quantum technologies with our pioneering cooling solutions</h2>



<div style="height:100px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div>



<div class="wp-block-columns is-layout-flex wp-container-core-columns-is-layout-8f761849 wp-block-columns-is-layout-flex">
<div class="wp-block-column is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow">
<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Quantum computing has the potential to address complex challenges in areas like drug development, battery technology and climate modeling. It will also enhance sensing capabilities, supporting applications in medical imaging and enabling physically secure quantum communication.<br> <br>Quantum devices can already be fabricated in large quantities using state-of-the-art thin-film deposition technology. However, developing and operating these devices requires extreme environments at temperatures well below 1 K (–272 °C). Dilution refrigerators usually generate these sub-kelvin temperatures using helium-3. This method is challenging and requires experienced personnel and complex infrastructure. As a result, dilution refrigeration leads to tedious low-temperature investigations and slow feedback on device properties and quality. This slows down quantum R&D. </p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The scarcity of helium-3 adds further risk. Helium-3 is one of the rarest resources on Earth and is produced in small volumes from radioactive tritium decay in mostly military facilities in North America and Russia. </p>
</div>



<div class="wp-block-column is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow">
<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Its extreme rarity and strategic relevance have even prompted considerations to mine helium-3 on the Moon, where it is embedded in the lunar rock. This limited availability threatens the sovereign use of quantum technologies and will hinder their advancement and adoption at industrial scale.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Because we believe that cryogenics should accelerate quantum technologies, not hold it back, we have dedicated ourselves to setting a new standard. This standard is characterized by high performance, fast delivery, absolute reliability and unmatched efficiency. Central to this is the use of a fundamentally different technology in our cryostats. To reach sub-kelvin temperatures, we cool with paramagnetic solids in a process known as <a href="https://kiutra.com/technology/#adr">Adiabatic Demagnetization Refrigeration (ADR)</a>.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">ADR is well established, but we are the only company worldwide that can offer it in a continuous configuration (cADR). With this, we provide the only alternative to helium-3-based cooling. Magnetic cooling has several advantages. Our cutting-edge cooling solutions are easy-to-use – you don’t need to hire a low-temperature physicist to run your cryostat. The systems are modular and fast, resulting in time, energy and cost savings.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div></div>
</div></div>



<div class="wp-block-cover" id="kiutra"><span aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-cover__background has-black-background-color has-background-dim-100 has-background-dim"></span><div class="wp-block-cover__inner-container is-layout-flow wp-block-cover-is-layout-flow"><div class="wp-block-image">
<figure class="aligncenter size-full"><img fetchpriority="high" decoding="async" width="737" height="475" alt="graphic explaining the company name: "Q or kju" the symbol for heat and "trahere" the latin for draw or extract, combining to make the name kiutra." class="wp-image-3983" data-lazy-srcset="https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/kiutra_expl_737x475px.jpg 737w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/kiutra_expl_737x475px-300x193.jpg 300w" data-lazy-sizes="(max-width: 737px) 100vw, 737px" data-lazy-src="https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/kiutra_expl_737x475px.jpg" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20737%20475'%3E%3C/svg%3E"><noscript><img fetchpriority="high" decoding="async" width="737" height="475" src="https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/kiutra_expl_737x475px.jpg" alt="graphic explaining the company name: "Q or kju" the symbol for heat and "trahere" the latin for draw or extract, combining to make the name kiutra." class="wp-image-3983" srcset="https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/kiutra_expl_737x475px.jpg 737w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/kiutra_expl_737x475px-300x193.jpg 300w" sizes="(max-width: 737px) 100vw, 737px" /></noscript></figure>
</div></div></div>



<div class="wp-block-cover is-light" id="Solutions"><span aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-cover__background has-white-background-color has-background-dim-100 has-background-dim"></span><div class="wp-block-cover__inner-container is-layout-constrained wp-block-cover-is-layout-constrained">
<div class="wp-block-group"><div class="wp-block-group__inner-container is-layout-constrained wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained">
<h2 class="wp-block-heading has-text-align-left">Advanced cooling solutions </h2>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading has-text-align-left">Enabling the development, characterization and testing as well as the operation of quantum systems</h3>



<div class="wp-block-columns is-layout-flex wp-container-core-columns-is-layout-8f761849 wp-block-columns-is-layout-flex">
<div class="wp-block-column is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow">
<h5 class="wp-block-heading">L-Type Rapid</h5>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The <a href="https://kiutra.com/products/l-type-rapid-cryostat/">L-Type Rapid</a> offers easy access to the kelvin and sub-kelvin range and delivers the fastest testing and characterization on the market, combining high sample throughput with ease of use.</p>



<h5 class="wp-block-heading">X-Type</h5>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The <a href="https://kiutra.com/products/x-type/">X-Type</a> is a modular, helium-3-free cooling architecture designed specifically for scalable quantum systems.</p>



<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="536" alt="L-Type Rapid fast characterization cryostat being loaded with a sample
" class="wp-image-4144" data-lazy-srcset="https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Sebastian-1200-x-628-1024x536.png 1024w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Sebastian-1200-x-628-300x157.png 300w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Sebastian-1200-x-628-768x402.png 768w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Sebastian-1200-x-628.png 1200w" data-lazy-sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" data-lazy-src="https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Sebastian-1200-x-628-1024x536.png" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201024%20536'%3E%3C/svg%3E"><noscript><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="536" src="https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Sebastian-1200-x-628-1024x536.png" alt="L-Type Rapid fast characterization cryostat being loaded with a sample
" class="wp-image-4144" srcset="https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Sebastian-1200-x-628-1024x536.png 1024w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Sebastian-1200-x-628-300x157.png 300w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Sebastian-1200-x-628-768x402.png 768w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Sebastian-1200-x-628.png 1200w" sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" /></noscript></figure>
</div>



<div class="wp-block-column is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow">
<h5 class="wp-block-heading">Specialized systems</h5>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">In addition, we develop cryogenic environments for specialized use cases. </p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">This includes the <a href="https://kiutra.com/products/cryostats-for-specialized-use-cases/#s-type-essential">S-Type</a>, a rack-integrated sub-kelvin platform for continuous cooling, and the T-Type, an optical kelvin temperature cryostat optimized for trapped-ion chips.</p>



<h5 class="wp-block-heading">Cryogenic Services</h5>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">To enable you to get fast testing and characterization results without the need for your own facility, we provide an on-demand <a href="https://kiutra.com/services">service</a>.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">You just need to send in a sample, and our dedicated low-temperature experts will then handle the entire process. This ensures cost-efficiency and swift, 100% reliable results for you. </p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Alternatively, set up and run your own experiments in our Characterization Lab, where we operate four dedicated L-Type Rapid cryostats alongside state-of-the-art measurement electronics.</p>
</div>
</div>



<div style="height:79px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div>



<div style="height:20px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading has-text-align-left">Shaping the future of quantum technologies</h2>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">We actively push innovation through research projects in cryogenics and quantum technologies</h3>



<div class="wp-block-columns is-layout-flex wp-container-core-columns-is-layout-8f761849 wp-block-columns-is-layout-flex">
<div class="wp-block-column is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow">
<p class="wp-block-paragraph">In 2024, we began work on two major R&D projects: LEMON and CRYOFAST. </p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph"><a href="https://kiutra.com/projects/large-scale-magnetic-cooling/">LEMON</a> focuses on developing a scalable, helium-3-free cryogenic cooling solution capable of reaching milli-Kelvin temperatures. By pushing the limits of cADR, LEMON will meet the increasing cooling demands of quantum technologies, especially in quantum computing. </p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph"><a href="https://kiutra.com/projects/cryofast-democratizing-access-to-sub-kelvin-temperatures/">CRYOFAST</a> aims to democratize access to sub-Kelvin temperatures by providing researchers and developers with professional testing and characterization services at a centralized facility for advanced cryogenic measurements on sensitive devices.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">We also collaborate with world-class partners, participating in numerous national and European research consortia. Our role in these initiatives involves developing and deploying integrated cryo-operating platforms. </p>
</div>



<div class="wp-block-column is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow">
<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="536" alt="Conducting a measurement on the cryo-floor " class="wp-image-4146" data-lazy-srcset="https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Klaus-1200-x-628-1024x536.png 1024w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Klaus-1200-x-628-300x157.png 300w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Klaus-1200-x-628-768x402.png 768w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Klaus-1200-x-628.png 1200w" data-lazy-sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" data-lazy-src="https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Klaus-1200-x-628-1024x536.png" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201024%20536'%3E%3C/svg%3E"><noscript><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="536" src="https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Klaus-1200-x-628-1024x536.png" alt="Conducting a measurement on the cryo-floor " class="wp-image-4146" srcset="https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Klaus-1200-x-628-1024x536.png 1024w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Klaus-1200-x-628-300x157.png 300w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Klaus-1200-x-628-768x402.png 768w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Klaus-1200-x-628.png 1200w" sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" /></noscript></figure>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Through our products and services, we cover the full spectrum of groundbreaking quantum technologies – from superconducting circuits, and ion traps for quantum computing to NV centers and single-photon detectors for sensing and communication applications. Looking ahead, our focus remains on providing systems, integrated solutions, and services across the entire quantum technology chain.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div></div>
</div></div>



<div class="wp-block-cover is-light" id="leadership"><span aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-cover__background has-grey-background-color has-background-dim-100 has-background-dim"></span><div class="wp-block-cover__inner-container is-layout-constrained wp-block-cover-is-layout-constrained">
<div class="wp-block-group"><div class="wp-block-group__inner-container is-layout-constrained wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained">
<h2 class="wp-block-heading has-text-align-left">Leadership</h2>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading has-text-align-left">Meet our management team</h3>
</div></div>



<div class="wp-block-group is-layout-grid wp-container-core-group-is-layout-4c3e5440 wp-block-group-is-layout-grid">

<div class="wp-block-cover is-light" id="sustainability"><span aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-cover__background has-white-background-color has-background-dim-100 has-background-dim"></span><div class="wp-block-cover__inner-container is-layout-flow wp-block-cover-is-layout-flow">
<div class="wp-block-group"><div class="wp-block-group__inner-container is-layout-flow wp-block-group-is-layout-flow">
<h2 class="wp-block-heading" id="sustainability">Sustainability</h2>



<div style="height:40px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div>



<div class="wp-block-columns is-layout-flex wp-container-core-columns-is-layout-8f761849 wp-block-columns-is-layout-flex">
<div class="wp-block-column is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow">
<p class="wp-block-paragraph">As scientists, we want to shape the world in a positive way and by taking a responsible approach to business operations, we want to do this as sustainably as we can. We have taken our first steps in assessing and reducing our greenhouse gas emissions (GHG) and see it as an essential area for continuous assessment and improvement.</p>


<div class="wp-block-image">
<figure class="aligncenter size-large is-resized"><img decoding="async" width="601" height="1024" alt="abstract colorful drawing of a tree" class="wp-image-4245" style="width:301px;height:512px" data-lazy-srcset="https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/GW_-Mockup_1-601x1024.jpg 601w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/GW_-Mockup_1-176x300.jpg 176w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/GW_-Mockup_1.jpg 654w" data-lazy-sizes="(max-width: 601px) 100vw, 601px" data-lazy-src="https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/GW_-Mockup_1-601x1024.jpg" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20601%201024'%3E%3C/svg%3E"><noscript><img decoding="async" width="601" height="1024" src="https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/GW_-Mockup_1-601x1024.jpg" alt="abstract colorful drawing of a tree" class="wp-image-4245" style="width:301px;height:512px" srcset="https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/GW_-Mockup_1-601x1024.jpg 601w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/GW_-Mockup_1-176x300.jpg 176w, https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/GW_-Mockup_1.jpg 654w" sizes="(max-width: 601px) 100vw, 601px" /></noscript></figure>
</div>


<p class="wp-block-paragraph"><strong>Climate impact pilot program</strong></p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">In 2022, we took part in a pilot program to model the climate impact of one of our cryostats compared to regular cryogenic equipment on the market today. The pilot showed that one L-Type Rapid cryostat can save up to 70% in energy use for a quick test (assuming 1 hour of testing time at 100 mK).</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph"></p>
</div>



<div class="wp-block-column is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow">
<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Importantly we also identified reduction of the GHG emissions from our electricity consumption as a top measure to implement at kiutra. We subsequently switched to a 100% green electricity supplier with electricity coming entirely from environmentally friendly hydropower plants.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph"><strong>Enabling quantum technologies</strong></p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">We build products and services to help scientists and engineers implement modern, second-generation quantum technologies. These technologies have the potential to provide significant benefits to the environment, for example by providing more energy-efficient computing, as well as disruptive computing that could lead to improved materials for energy storage and conversion, solar cell and battery technology, and power distribution.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph"><strong>Long lasting and recyclable products</strong> </p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Our products are designed to last – they are durable, repairable, and upgradable. This reduces waste and saves energy and resources that would otherwise be used in the production of a brand-new cryostat or to replace a substantial part of the device.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph"><strong>3D printing</strong></p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">In development and various production processes we use 3D printing as a prototyping tool to cut down the production stages needed for metal parts, reducing the energy used and emissions produced to build them.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph"><strong>What we are working on</strong></p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">We are currently engaged in a project to help evaluate processes and to identify and quantify areas where we can lessen our environmental impact.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div></div>
